12. The Accountant and the GSO to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for all PPE accounts to have a reliable source of information on their existence.

16. Set a definite timeline for the completion of the land tax Computerization program; development.
17. Comply with the issuance of a Notice of Delinquency for delinquent taxes in accordance with Section 254 of RA No. 7160; and
18. Implement a more effective policy in handling long outstanding delinquent accounts through the issuance of the Warrant of Levy or through legal action in accordance with existing laws and regulations.

We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to increase of the fidelity bonds of the City’s AOs assigned in the Land Tax Division commensurate with their accountabilities and in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the City’s resources.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to:
21. Require the immediate liquidation of cash advances especially those aging two years and below in the total amount of ₱0.781 million to avoid the transactions being suspended in audit; and
22. Withhold the salaries of officers and employees who did not liquidate their cash advances after the third and final demand in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that positions whose duties and responsibilities are considered incompatible duties should not be handled by a single person in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the management ensure full implementation of the project within 1 year from actual delivery and acceptance of the E-Trike units to avoid revocation of the donation; require the E-Trike project manager to reassess the DOE E-Trike Development Plan and the feasibility of the project as a whole, considering the low deployment ratio and the significant delay in its implementation.

We recommended that the management require the officer in charge of E-trike to recall all units deployed to administrative offices and other government agencies and ensure that these units are released to intended beneficiaries in compliance with the DOD and the DOE E-Trike Development Plan. Otherwise, hold the officer in charge responsible for the release of 13 E-Trike units without the necessary supporting documents and required authority.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Assessor to prepare an updated Summary of Market Values (SMV) and cause the drafting of an appropriate ordinance for consideration of the Sanggunian in compliance with the provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Mayor strictly impose the policy that all payments shall be made through checks directly issued to the suppliers or creditors to whom the money is due in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 unless it is very difficult, impractical or impossible to do so.

1.6. We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:

RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Card while the implementation of the
property tax computerization program is still ongoing;
1.6.2. Treasurer to create a duly certified list showing the name of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based
on the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Card at the beginning of each
year, and furnish this list to the Accountant as basis for recording
entries in the RPT/SET Receivable accounts;
1.6.3. Accountant to draft journal entry vouchers to correct the
erroneous entries in RPT/SET Receivables amounting to ₱35.313
million; and
1.6.4. Accountant and the Treasurer to establish a system for the regular
monitoring and reconciliation of RPT/SET Receivables to ensure
accuracy in the financial records.

budget planning process for succeeding years; and

issuance of a budget ordinance by the Sanggunian in cases where
actual repairs are found to be capitalizable upon implementation.

December 1, 2025; and

Agency Payables and Trust Liabilities accounts so that erroneous
accounting entries are detected/corrected in a timely manner or
implement other compensating controls to ensure that fund
transfers received are correctly recorded.

We recommended that the City Mayor institute and maintain sound and effective internal control measures in disbursement process involving the confidential fund to ensure that:
1. The original specimen signature of signatories to DV and ObR are submitted in accordance with the regulation;

Submit the bank reconciliation statements and supporting documents for the Cash in Bank accounts with a total amount of ₱3,000,000.00 as of December 31, 2020 so that the accuracy and reliability of the reported balances can be validated;

Assess the necessity in maintaining these accounts separately, and consider transferring it to the main depository account.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to create an inventory committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical count of PPE in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

Require the adoption of a uniform property identification system for PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that DVs and supporting documents
are submitted within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular
No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Accountant submit all BRS due for submission in
compliance with Section 3.4 of COA Circular 96-011. Henceforth, the City Accountant
must adhere strictly to the aforementioned regulation by submitting the monthly BRS
after the end of each month.

We recommended that the City Treasurer ensure that all collections be deposited intact daily
or not later than the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs
Volume 1, and that payment of land taxes on the succeeding year be recorded as
collections for that year.

We recommended immediate liquidation of all unliquidated CAs in compliance with the
provision of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical inventory
taking, accomplish and submit the RPCPPE, and update and reconcile the related
subsidiary records to have an assurance that the amount of PPE reported in the
financial statement is fairly presented.

Henceforth, physical inventory count and reconciliation of subsidiary records shall be
regularly undertaken to ensure the existence and accuracy of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Accountant strictly adhere to the
guidelines on the utilization of the 20% DF and include only those programs and
projects that are in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Local School Board minimize spending for items not
considered as priority programs under SEF in compliance with Section 3 of DepEd-
DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We further recommended that should non-priority program be included in the Local
School Board Budget, it shall be net of the budgetary provisions for the same or related
item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council.

We recommended that the Local School Board and the City Mayor ensure that SEF
shall not be spent on items which are not among the allowable expenses under Section
272 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160.

We further recommended that SEF expenditures totaling ₱900,000.00, which were spent
for “The DepEd of La Carlota City Seal of Excellence,” be refunded for lack of legal basis.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council maximize the utilization of the Fund for
pre-disaster activities and projects to have an assurance that the City is adequately
prepared to protect its constituent and respond to harmful effects of disaster.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that the exact amounts of taxes
withheld are remitted within ten (10) days after the end of each month in compliance
with the Section 5 of RR No. 17-2003.

We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that all payments out of government
funds are made through checks in compliance with COA Circular 97-002 unless it is
very difficult, impractical or impossible to do so.

We recommended that the responsible officers and employees refund the payments of
registration fees in excess of the allowable amount.

We further recommended that the City Mayor shall not approve cash advances for
conventions, trainings and seminars and other similar activities with registration fees
exceeding ₱2,000.00 per personnel per day in compliance with Section 3 of NBC No. 563.

We recommended that the CSWDO and the BUB Focal Person undertake immediately
the implementation of the projects under BUB 2015 and BUB 2016 so that the SLP‟s
objective could be attained.

We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that at least 1% of the IRA shall be
allocated for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the LCPC in
compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee:

a. Conduct complete physical inventory count of all properties and accomplish and
submit the RPCPPE to ensure that PPE accounts are fairly presented in the
Statement of Financial Position.

b. Reconcile the result of physical count with the balance per books and prepare
necessary adjusting entries to correct the differences noted.

Physical inventory count and reconciliation of subsidiary records be henceforth
regularly undertaken to ensure the existence and accuracy of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Property and Supply Officer ensure that unserviceable
properties are timely disposed of in compliance with Section 79 of PD 1445. Such
disposal shall be undertaken in accordance with the Manual on the Disposal of
Government Property or National Budget Circular No. 425 dated January 28, 1992.

We recommended that the College Treasurer ensure that the exact amounts of taxes
withheld are remitted within ten (10) days after the end of each month in compliance
with the Section 5 of RR No. 17-2003.

We recommended that the outstanding health insurance premiums be remitted
immediately. For succeeding periods, the College Treasurer should ensure that the
health insurance premiums withheld should be remitted in full within ten (10) days
after the end of each month in compliance with Section 20 Paragraph b of the IRR of
RA 7875 as amended by RA 9241.

We recommended that the College President ensure that at least 5% of the Annual
Budget be appropriated for GAD in compliance with Section 4.4 of DBM-NEDA-
NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-001.

We further recommended that GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report be
promptly submitted to enable timely verification of GAD related PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that DVs and supporting
documents are submitted within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular
No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Accountant submit all BRS due for submission in
compliance with Section 3.4 of COA Circular 96-011. Henceforth, the City Accountant
must adhere faithfully to the aforementioned regulation by submitting the monthly BRS
after the end of each month.

We recommended that no official receipt shall be issued before the actual receipt of
payment and the City Treasurer must ensure that all collections be deposited intact
daily or not later than the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the MNGAS for
LGUs Volume 1.

We also recommended that payment of land taxes on the succeeding year must be
recorded as collections for that year.

We recommended that the City Treasurer ensures that all cash advances granted
to Accountable Officers shall not exceed their maximum cash accountability in
compliance with Section 305 (f) of R.A. 7160 and Section 7.3 of COA Circular
No. 97-002.

The City Treasurer may increase the Fidelity Bonds of Accountable Officers to
an amount sufficient to cover their maximum cash accountabilities that they handle for
a given period based on the schedule provided under Section I of Treasury Circular
No. 02-009 dated August 6, 2009 to protect the interest of the City in case of loss from
the unlawful use of government funds and those attributable to negligence in the
safekeeping of such.

We recommended that the City Mayor demand immediate liquidation/settlement
of unliquidated cash advances especially those granted December 31, 2011 and below
considering that a final notice and demand to all accountable officers must have been
issued pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-04.

Moreover, the City Accountant conduct periodic evaluation and validation of all
unliquidated cash advance. If necessary, he may request for the write-off of dormant
cash advances following the procedures of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

The City Mayor send a follow up letter to ICFAU inquiring as to the status of
liquidation reports previously submitted pertaining to confidential and intelligence fund.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensures that cash advances for
payroll shall be equal to the net amount of payroll for the pay period in compliance with
Section 4.2 of COA Circular 97-002.

We further recommended that grant of cash advances for payroll shall be
supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 1.1.1 of COA
Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the City Mayor refrain from approving any fund transfer to NGO if
the disbursement is not supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 2.1 of
COA Circular 2012-001 dated July 14, 2012 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We also recommended that the City Accountant must see to it that release of fund transfer
to NGOs should be supported with complete documentation enumerated under COA
Circular No. 2012-001. Further, he must require the NGO to submit Liquidation reports
and supporting documents 60 days after the completion of the activity or project being
implemented.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct complete physical
inventory count, accomplish and submit the RPCPPE to have and assurance that the
amount of PPE in the financial statements is fairly presented.

Henceforth, physical inventory count and reconciliation of subsidiary records
shall be regularly undertaken to ensure the existence and accuracy of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the City Planning and Development Office formulate
comprehensive annual development plans and refrain from appropriating funds to
projects stated in generic terms in accordance to Section 106 of RA 7160.

Should generic appropriations be unavoidable, the City Accountant shall ensure
that subsequent expenditures are made only if prior approval was given by the
Sangguniang Panlungsod pursuant to Section 305 (b) of RA 7160.

We recommended the following to the members of the Local School Board;
a. To refrain from utilizing the SEF for payment of expenses that are not
priority program, projects and activities in compliance to section 363(c) of the
IRR of RA 7160.
b. To submit DepEd CY 2016 School Budget to justify the payment of the above
expenses by the School Board due to deficient budget in the DepEd.
c. In the preparation of the School Board Budget priority shall be given to
program, activities and projects enumerated in Section 363(c) of the IRR of
RA 7160 and must utilize the proceeds of the additional one percent SEF to
such purpose prescribed by the law for which SEF can be disposed of.

We recommended that the School Board refrain from utilizing the SEF fund for
payment of expenses that are not priority program, projects and activities in compliance
to Section 363(c) of the IRR of RA 7160.

We recommended also that the members of the Local School Board, in the
preparation of the School Board budget should give priority to programs, activities and
projects enumerated in Section 363(c) of the IRR of RA 7160 and must utilize the
proceeds of the additional one percent SEF to such purpose prescribed by law for which
SEF can be disposed of.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council prepare and update the annual
investment plan on DRRM pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 guidelines.

We also recommended that the Council maximize the utilization of the Fund for
pre-disaster activities and projects to have an assurance that the City is well prepared in
case disaster may strike the City.

We recommended that:

a. The City submit Evaluation Reports or any documentation recognizing
suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts
which resulted in monetary savings making the employees eligible for
PRAISE award to warrant the grant of SEI.
b. Report detailing the savings generated during CY 2016 which prompted the
grant of SEI and relevant supporting documents.
c. In the event that the City fails to submit the above mentioned requirements or
submit specific legal basis for the grant of the incentives, it recommended that
the amount be refunded by the City government officials and employees for lack
of specific legal authority.
